SEARCH
Package com.blackaby.Backend.Emulation.CPU

Enum Class DuckCPU.Register

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<DuckCPU.Register>
com.blackaby.Backend.Emulation.CPU.DuckCPU.Register
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<DuckCPU.Register>, Constable
Enclosing class:
DuckCPU
public static enum DuckCPU.Register extends Enum<DuckCPU.Register>
Identifies 8-bit and 16-bit CPU registers used by the decoder and instruction helpers.

    GetRegFrom2Bit(int bitId, boolean isAfContext)
    Decodes the two-bit register-pair field used by 16-bit instructions.

    Decodes the three-bit register field used by many byte instructions.

    • values

      public static DuckCPU.Register[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static DuckCPU.Register valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • GetRegFrom3Bit

      public static DuckCPU.Register GetRegFrom3Bit(int bitId)
      Decodes the three-bit register field used by many byte instructions.
      Parameters:
      bitId - encoded register field
      Returns:
      decoded register

    • GetRegFrom2Bit

      public static DuckCPU.Register GetRegFrom2Bit(int bitId, boolean isAfContext)
      Decodes the two-bit register-pair field used by 16-bit instructions.
      Parameters:
      bitId - encoded register field
      isAfContext - whether field `11` should resolve to `AF` instead of `SP`
      Returns:
      decoded register pair
